section1.title: What is website redesign?
section1.p1: Redesign is the process of updating an existing website: visual style, structure, usability, and technology. It's not just about making it prettier — it's rebuilding the product to match current business goals and user behavior.
section1.p2: An outdated site loses trust, performs poorly on mobile, loads slowly, and fails to generate leads. A redesign addresses these issues systematically.

section2.title: When is it time to update your site?
items: Your site is 3–4+ years old and looks outdated
Conversions have dropped or you're getting fewer leads
The site doesn't display well on mobile devices
Slow loading — users leave before the page appears
Your services, positioning, or target audience have changed
The site is difficult to edit or update
Competitors look more modern and professional
section2.footer: If at least two points apply — it's time for a refresh.

section3.title: What do you get?
items: A modern responsive design aligned with current trends
Integration with CRM, analytics, and other services

section4.title: How we work
section4.p1: We start with an audit of your current site: analyzing structure, speed, usability, search rankings, and user behavior. We determine what to keep and what to rebuild.
section4.p2: Then we design the new structure, create the visual design, and migrate to a modern platform. Content is transferred carefully, and SEO positions are preserved through redirects and technical optimization.
section4.p3: After launch, we monitor metrics and refine as needed.

section5.title: Technology
section5.p1: We use a modern stack: Vue / Nuxt, React, Next.js, WordPress, Webflow — the choice depends on the task and project scope.
section5.p2: All sites are optimized for Core Web Vitals, mobile-ready, and prepared for marketing.

partial.label: Incremental improvements
pros: Minimal impact on current site operations

redesignCalculator.title: Redesign cost calculator
redesignCalculator.totalNote: Estimated cost. Exact pricing after discussing your project.

redesignFaq.q: How is a redesign different from building a site from scratch?
redesignFaq.a: A redesign builds on your existing site: we keep what works — content, SEO rankings, and functional elements — while updating the look, structure, and technology. It's faster and more affordable than starting from zero.

redesignFaq.q: How long does a redesign take?
redesignFaq.a: On average 2 to 6 weeks. A visual refresh is quicker; a full rebuild with migration takes longer.

redesignFaq.q: Will I lose my search rankings?
redesignFaq.a: No, if done correctly. We set up redirects, preserve URL structure, and optimize technical SEO parameters.

redesignFaq.q: Do I need to rewrite all the content?
redesignFaq.a: Not necessarily. We can migrate existing content, but we recommend updating copy if it's outdated or no longer matches your positioning.

redesignFaq.q: Can you update only the design without changing the platform?
redesignFaq.a: Yes. We can refresh the visual layer while keeping your current CMS or framework. However, if the platform is outdated, we recommend a full rebuild.

redesignFaq.q: What happens to the old site during the process?
redesignFaq.a: Your old site continues to work. We develop the new version in parallel and switch over only when everything is ready.

redesignFaq.q: Will you set up analytics and CRM?
redesignFaq.a: Yes. We configure Google Analytics, ad platform pixels, CRM, and other integrations as needed.

redesignFaq.q: How much does a redesign cost?
redesignFaq.a: Cost depends on scope, number of pages, and additional features. You can get a preliminary estimate using our calculator.
